default.description: Default code review rubric — applied to Stage 3 implementation reviews

items.label: Correctness
items.criteria: Does the implementation do what the spec requires? Are edge cases handled? Input validation present?

items.label: Completeness
items.criteria: Are all specified requirements implemented? No missing features or skipped ACs?

items.label: Quality
items.criteria: Is the code well-structured, readable, and maintainable? Follows project patterns? No magic numbers or deep chains?

documentation.description: Documentation review rubric — applied when reviewing REQUESTS.md, PROGRESS.md, RETROSPECTIVE.md, IMPLEMENTATION.md

items.label: Clarity
items.criteria: Is the document clear, unambiguous, and free of contradictions? Does it use precise language?

items.label: Completeness
items.criteria: Does the document cover all required sections? Are all FRs/ACs/phases addressed?

items.label: Actionability
items.criteria: Can an implementer act on this document without asking follow-up questions? Are file paths, commands, and expected outputs specified?

planning.description: Planning review rubric — applied to PROGRESS.md at S2→S3 AK gate

items.label: Phase Completeness
items.criteria: Every phase has goals, file changes, AC checkboxes, agent assignment, and wave/dependency metadata. 4+ file phases are split into Steps.

items.label: AC Reference Validity
items.criteria: Each phase references at least one AC from REQUESTS.md. AC checkboxes are testable per Spec Granularity Rules (running X produces Y).

items.label: Feasibility & File References
items.criteria: File references resolve to existing files (or new files marked Create). Phase dependencies are correctly ordered. Risks are present.
